faq.question: How much should elastic be cut?
faq.answer: Cut elastic to 75-90% of the body measurement, depending on application. Waistband: 85%. Cuffs: 80%. These are starting points — test on scrap first, as elastic stretch varies widely by brand and width.

faq.question: Which elastic width should I use?
faq.answer: 1/4": necklines, sleeve gathering. 1/2": lightweight garments, cuffs. 3/4"-1": standard waistbands. 1.5"-2": heavy-duty waistbands, pajamas. Wider elastic distributes pressure and is more comfortable for waistbands.

faq.question: Why does my elastic twist in the casing?
faq.answer: Common causes: elastic casing too wide (should be elastic width + 1/4"), elastic not tacked at seam lines, or using braided elastic (which narrows when stretched and twists). Use knit or woven elastic for casings — it maintains width when stretched.

faq.question: Is the Elastic Calculator free to use?

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Elastic Calculator?
sections.body: The Elastic Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your body measurement, elastic type, elastic width, you get instant results including cut elastic to, including seam allowance, tip. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting cut elastic to right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct.
